persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for someone who wants high-status, interesting things to attend:
- Oyster happy hours or upscale food/drink (wine dinners, tastings, chef's tables)
- Galas, fundraisers, charity benefits, receptions, award ceremonies
- Events where they can attend as a subject matter expert: public records law, open meeting law, FOIA, government transparency, civic accountability, journalism
- High-status professional networking, panels, policy forums, finance/VC/startup events
- Fashion/model events, runway shows, press events, media events, bikini/swimwear shows, fitness model showcases, promotional model events, brand activations
- Recurring upscale nightlife: scene-y bar/club nights, VIP events, boat/yacht cruises, influencer events
- Anything exclusive, influential, or scene-y

SCORE 9-10 (always attend):
- "Fashionably LATE" Thursdays at The Liberty Hotel — weekly interactive runway, models weave through crowd, free admission
- Boston Fashion Week events (Oct 4-10, 2026) and surrounding pre/post events
- SwimBoston — annual swimwear/bikini runway at Boston Harbor Distillery (21+)
- Boston En Vogue runway shows — luxury evening gowns and couture
- Mass Fashion Week showcases
- Harpoon Brewery festivals (St. Pats, Summer Games at Lawn on D, Oktoberfest)
- Boat/yacht cruises on Boston Harbor — Taylor Swift, Barbie, themed nights
- Any event at Royale Boston, ICON, VENU with 6ONE7 Productions or similar promoters
- Museum/gallery opening receptions with a cocktail hour or wine — Harvard Art Museums, MFA, ICA, Gardner — not a regular museum visit but a special evening event with a crowd
- University cultural events open to the public that draw an interesting mix of people (academics, creatives, professionals) — Harvard, MIT, BU, Northeastern

NOT wanted: pure sports games, road races, youth sports, basic trivia nights at chains.

events.name: In-Person Weekly Hack Night - First Tuesday
events.description:
Code for Boston is now running In-Person meetups every first and third Tuesday, with Zoom option. Other Tuesdays, we convene in Zoom. We also offer new member orientation both in-person and on Zoom.
Join us for civic volunteering with project champions from local government, non-profits, and the like!
If you are going to attend the virtual event, please make sure to do the following, and then join us on Zoom at 7pm EST:

* Join our Slack: [http://slack.codeforboston.org](http://slack.codeforbost
events.reason: Code for Boston civic volunteering—perfect match for Brian's government transparency and open government interests, meaningful civic engagement

events.name: Discussion: U.S. Regional Divisions
events.description: Why do different parts of the United States feel—and function—so differently? From state to state and region to region, people experience civic life in ways that reflect much more than political preferences. These differences are shaped by a wide mix of factors: economic conditions, geography and climate, local laws and policies, religious traditions, historical experiences, demographic shifts, and sociocultural values. Together, they help define the identities and perspectives of communities ac
events.reason: Discussion on U.S. Regional Divisions—directly relevant to civic understanding, policy, demographics, and regional governance—aligns with Brian's public affairs interests

events.name: April: AI workflows at scale + K8s how-to
events.description:
[Coder](coder.com) is sponsoring food & bev this time - thank you **Coder!!**

As usual:

1. **RSVP's close 48 hrs before the event**. Please make it easy on us by RSVP'ing only if you intend to show up.
2. **Don't be late!** You'll get locked out, and that's no fun!
===

**Agenda**

6:00 pm: food / drinks / networking
6:20 pm: Talk #1: **AI Workflows At Scale** with Nicky Pike from Coder
7:00 pm: Who is hiring? Who is looking?
7:10 pm: Talk #2: **Deploy LiteLLM on K8s** with Michael O'Leary
7:5
events.reason: Tech networking with food/drinks sponsored by Coder—developer/startup crowd, AI and K8s talks, good professional networking

events.description:
*Please RSVP ahead of time and bring a government-issued ID for check-in.*

A talk on how to operate within a tech company in ways that help you stand out and grow in your career. This is especially important in 2026 as the traditional work of developers gets pushed to AI. What are the important contributions someone can bring to a tech company? This talk explores several things you have to pay attention to in order to grow your career.

About the Speaker
Rafael Mendiola, co-founder, building a
events.reason: Tech career panel on standing out and growing—applicable to Brian's tech interests, thought leadership format

events.name: Agents at Work
events.description:
Whether you're building with AI every day or just starting to explore what's possible, **Agents at Work** is the evening for you. This is a free, in-person event bringing together professionals, builders, and enthusiasts across the tech and AI landscape for an evening of insightful conversation and real human connection.
Join us at **Microsoft NERD in Boston** — for an expert panel discussion followed by open networking. No matter your role, background, or experience level, this is a space where
events.reason: Agents at Work at Microsoft NERD — in-person tech networking at a prestigious venue with expert panel and professionals; strong crowd and meaningful conversation.

restaurants.name: Zuma
restaurants.editorial: High-energy Japanese izakaya in the Seaport. Known for a lively bar scene, beautiful room, and a crowd that dresses up. Popular for late dinner and drinks.

restaurants.name: Desnuda
restaurants.editorial: Intimate raw bar and natural wine spot. Industry crowd, low-key cool. East Boston location and Seaport outpost.
